items.final_wording: Supports asking about the details of a fall when a covered person presents after one and explicitly rejects automatic prediction-tool use.

source.title: Falls: assessment and prevention in older people and in people 50 and over at higher risk
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; requires UAE pathway adaptation
source.population: People aged 65 years and over, and people aged 50 to 64 with one or more factors increasing fall risk.
source.setting: Community settings including emergency departments and minor injury units, and hospital inpatient settings.
source.exclusions: Exact for asking details of a fall and for comprehensive clinician assessment of specified falls-risk domains in the covered age group. It does not cover every traumatic injury, younger adults or children, and it must not auto-score fall risk.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.5 — identifying people for falls assessment

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports asking about the details of a fall when a covered person presents after one and explicitly rejects automatic prediction-tool use.

items.rationale: Supports entered fall history and identification for falls assessment for falls-related injury review; no clinical decision is generated.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician assessment of cardiovascular, cognition, delirium, diet/fluid, dizziness, footwear, function, gait/balance, hearing, long-term conditions, medicines, neurological, osteoporosis, continence and vision domains when relevant.

source.title: Falls: assessment and prevention in older people and in people 50 and over at higher risk
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; requires UAE pathway adaptation
source.population: People aged 65 years and over, and people aged 50 to 64 with one or more factors increasing fall risk.
source.setting: Community settings including emergency departments and minor injury units, and hospital inpatient settings.
source.exclusions: Exact for asking details of a fall and for comprehensive clinician assessment of specified falls-risk domains in the covered age group. It does not cover every traumatic injury, younger adults or children, and it must not auto-score fall risk.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician assessment of cardiovascular, cognition, delirium, diet/fluid, dizziness, footwear, function, gait/balance, hearing, long-term conditions, medicines, neurological, osteoporosis, continence and vision domains when relevant.

items.rationale: Supports clinician-led comprehensive falls assessment for falls-related injury review; no clinical decision is generated.

items.final_wording: Covers non-complex fracture assessment and follow-up, repeated pain assessment, definitive X-ray reporting, Ottawa ankle and foot rules, and selected scaphoid imaging.

source.title: Fractures (non-complex): assessment and management — Recommendations
source.population: Children and adults with non-complex fractures managed in emergency or orthopaedic services.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected non-complex fracture assessment, documentation, imaging, weight-bearing, information, and follow-up contexts; partial for a generic fracture-follow-up workflow and not validated for UAE pathways.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Covers non-complex fracture assessment and follow-up, repeated pain assessment, definitive X-ray reporting, Ottawa ankle and foot rules, and selected scaphoid imaging.

items.rationale: Supports entered non-complex fracture assessment context for falls-related injury review; no clinical decision is generated.
